summary: Three major AI-related workforce reductions announced April 25-26: Meta's 8,000 cuts (10% workforce) starting May 20, Microsoft's voluntary buyouts targeting 15,000 (7% of staff), and Nike's 1,400 tech-focused layoffs. Combined displacement: 24,400 jobs eliminated.

events.headline: Meta announces 8,000 job cuts effective May 20 as part of AI efficiency push
events.excerpt: Meta announced on April 26 that it will cut approximately 8,000 jobs—roughly 10% of its workforce—starting May 20, 2026.

events.headline: Microsoft offers voluntary buyouts to 7% of staff amid AI restructuring
events.excerpt: Microsoft said Thursday it would offer buyouts to 7% of staff.

events.headline: Nike announces 1,400 job cuts concentrated in technology department
events.excerpt: Nike announced a new round of layoffs Thursday affecting approximately 1,400 employees across the company, mostly concentrated in its technology department.
